Qt6
CiWof Salinas
OFFICE OF THE CITY ATTORNEY . 200 Lincoln Avenue . Salinas, Catifornia 93901 . (408) 758.7256
October 6, 1992
: 
'"32
JiI1 Stecher, Counsel
Legal Division 
.l
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
sacramento, california 
95812
RE: REQUEST FOR ADVICE
FILE NO. A-92-550
Dear Ms. Stecher:
In reviewing your advice letter with Councilmember Caballero, she
asked that I provide further clarification regarding the second
question posed in my letter dated August 4, J-992 in order to obtain
additional advice.
Irts. Caballero and I understand the award of the maintenance
contract may invoJ.ve a conflict of interest depending on her
economic interests vis-a-vis the person or business entity
receiving the contract. The thrust of my question, however, was
focused on the Council's preliminary decision whether or not to
contract the work to outside contractors which must be decided
prior to the Council receiving specific information about the
particular contracting person or business entity.
In other words, does Ms. Caballero have a conflict of interest when
determining the preliminary question of rrhether the work should be
contracted through outside services or whether the City crer., should
do the work? The decision whether to have the maintenance work
perforned by City staff or by outside contractors could have an
impact on the maintenance cost, but not necessarily, if the City
and outside contractor costs are egua1.
I hope that this correspondence adequately clarifies my second
question. I an available to provide additional information, if
needed, and I thank you for your continuing assistance and
courtesies.
Sincerely,
CITY ATTORNEY
cc: Councilmember Anna Caballero
I




Ciryof Salinas
OFFICE OF THE CITY ATTORNEY . 200 Lincoln Avenue . Salinas. Catifornia 93901 .
758-7256
August 4, 1992
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807
RE: REOUEST FOR FORI4AL WRITTEN ADVICE
Fd.,L
AU'I
I
z'99
Dear Commission:
Pursuant to Government Code section 83114(b) and section 18329 of
the California Code of Regulations, formal rrritte advice is hereby
requested as follows:
a. This request is made on behalf of Anna Caballero,
Councilmember of the City of salinas, c/o 200 Lincoln Avenue,
Salinas, CA 93901. Ms. Caballero has authorized Stephanie Atigh,
Salinas City Attorney, to submit this request for formal written
advice to determine if she is disqualified from participating in or
making decisions pursuant to the Political Refortn Act,
b. The material facts for this request are as follows:
Ms. Caballero has been an elected mernber of the City Council of
Salinas continuously since June, 1991. She and her husband own
real estate at 941 New Sa1em Drive in Salinas. This real estate is
located within the North East Assessment District, established by
the City Council in 1989, to acquire and construct public
improvements such as streets, curbs, gutters and sidewalks, street
lights, storm drains, sanitary sewers, parks and open space. Ms.
Caballero's real estate is also located in the North East
Maintenance District which was established in 1990 for the purpose
of maintaining the public improvements acquired and constructed by
the North East Assessment District.
The City Councj-1 is required to make several determinations
annually regarding the North East Assessment District. First, the
Council is asked to determine how the maintenance work will be
performed, specifical.ly, whether the work will be performed by
in-house City staff or by outside contractors through competitive
bid. rf the council determines that the maintenanc6 work should be
done by contract, the Council is then asked to confirm the plans
and specifications of the proposed maintenance r.rork and to
advertise for bids to be returned by a specific date. Those bids
are l-ater brought to the council for a decision regarding the award
of the contract.
-1-




In addition, the assessments for the North East Maintenance
District are reviewed annually through a publ-ic hearing process at
which the Council is asked to confirm the assessments and levy
assessments for that fiscal year. Those assessments are then
levied against each parcel of land in the District, including the
real estate owned by Ms. Caballero, to be collected with the
paynent of real property taxes in two annual installments.
l,ls. Caballero has abstained from participating in any decision
regarding the annual assessments for the North East Maintenance
District. 
She has also recently abstained from a Council decision
to have the maintenance work perforned by City staff as opposed to
an independent contractor. Ms. Caballero has expressed her
intention not to participate in any decision regarding the North
East Assessment District or North East l,laintenance District pending
this request for formal written advice from the Commission.
f arn available to provide additional information as needed.
Thank you for your assistance with this request.
Sincerely,w^w
CITY ATTORNEY
cc: Councilmember Anna Caballero
-2-
